root_cause: A gateway release tightened resume validation after shard transfers and rejected resume tokens whose generation lagged the newly moved shard state. During a scheduled shard rebalance, that change invalidated a large set of otherwise recoverable client sessions, forcing full reconnects. The resulting identify surge saturated auth and session-store dependencies, which looked like primary failures but were downstream effects of a reconnect storm.

details: Resume generation mismatch and stale nonce rejections begin immediately after the deployment and rebalance overlap
Redis and auth warnings appear after reconnect and identify amplification has already started
gateway.resume_rejection_percent and identify_requests_per_min spike before session-store CPU and auth latency peak
edge packet loss remains normal, weakening the regional network hypothesis
Commit c1827ef changed resume token handling around shard generation mismatches
Commit ea92b13 tightened identify limiter behavior, worsening the recovery path under reconnect load
The rebalance starts first, then the gateway rollout lands, and the incident begins only once both conditions overlap
Only sessions touched by shard transfers showed elevated resume rejection early, which distinguishes the incident from a generic gateway fleet bug
realtime-gateway, session-store, auth-api, and client-relay degrade in sequence rather than simultaneously

recommended_actions: Make session resume tolerant of generation drift during controlled shard transfer windows
Add reconnect storm circuit breakers that protect auth and session-store from identify amplification
Prevent control-plane rebalances and resume-path deployments from overlapping without canary validation
expected_investigation_path: Start with disconnect and reconnect telemetry rather than assuming edge instability
Construct the sequence across gateway, auth, and session-store to separate primary from secondary failures
Review commits and operational events touching shard transfer and resume semantics
Validate the RCA by correlating the overlapping rebalance plus deployment timing and rollback recovery
